data.title: China's Capital Market May Play Bigger Role in Supporting Tech Innovation, Industrial Upgrades — Market Talk
data.description: China's capital market will likely play a bigger role in supporting technological innovation and industrial upgrades, UBS analysts write in a note. Homegrown AI startup DeepSeek has brought global investors' attention to Chinese innovation, they say. The broader tech sector's share of overall A-share market capitalization has risen over the past decade to reach 21%, the analysts note. Industries that are more correlated with the AI theme, such as computers, automobiles and electronics, had notable reratings after the Lunar New Year holiday, they add. In the medium term, AI-related stocks could surge as fundamental tailwinds gain momentum and more use cases emerge, the analysts say. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: LIC Housing Finance to issue 5-year bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's LIC Housing Finance plans to raise 24 billion rupees ($276.13 million), including a greenshoe option of 14 billion rupees, through a sale of bonds maturing in five years, three bankers said on Tuesday.

The housing finance company has invited bids from bankers and investors for the issue on Thursday, they said.

LIC Housing Finance did not reply to a Reuters email for comment.

Here is the list of deals reported so far on February 18:

data.title: Buyer Exercises Option to Purchase Natural Cool Property
data.description:
Natural Cool Holdings said ClosetDesign has exercised its option to purchase a property from the company, located at 9 Kaki Bukit Road Eunos Technolink, Singapore.

This follows the company's initial announcement on Feb. 5 regarding the proposed SG$1.1 million sale.

With the exercise of the option, Natural Cool has received 5% of the total consideration.

data.title: Stocks making the biggest moves at noon: Judo Capital, HMC, Hub24 and more
data.description:
These are the ASX companies and sectors making headlines in afternoon trade.

Judo Capital – Shares rallied 10% after the company reported a better-than-expected set of 1H25 results, supported by lower impairments and solid net interest margins. The key highlights include:

  • Pre-provision profit up 7% half-on-half to $85.5 million or a 3% miss vs. consensus of $88.1 million

  • Cash earnings up 70% half-on-half to $40.9 million or 13% ahead of $36.3 million consensus

  • Net interest margin of 2.81% or 2 basis points below consensus

  • Net interest margin guidance upgraded to upper end of 2.90% to 3.00% for FY25, with an exit NIM of ~3%

HMC Capital – Shares soared 12.5% after the alternative asset manager posted a strong 1H25 result, driven by a 45% half-on-half increase in assets under management to $18.5 billion. The key highlights include (vs. Jarden estimates):

  • Pre-tax earnings per share up 204% to 51.9 cents vs. 37.4 cents estimates

  • Underlying earnings per share up 218% to 15 cents, in-line with estimates as lower investment income and higher corporate expenses offset higher management fees

  • Interim dividend of 6 cents per share, in-line with consensus

Hub24 – Shares rallied 6.5% but pulled back from session highs of 13.8% after the company posted a mixed 1H25 result. The stock may be under pressure due to valuation concerns and the ~130% run in the past twelve months.

  • Revenue up 24.6% to $195.2 million (2.5% beat vs. consensus)

  • EBITDA up 41.1% to $77.6 million (5.4% beat)

  • Adjusted NPAT up 40.1% to $42.6 million (0.6% miss)

  • Dividend up 29.7% to 24 cents per share (2.6% miss)

  • Upgraded FY26 funds under management guidance to $123-135 billion (from $115-123 billion)

Monadelphous – Shares rose 5.8% after the company posted a strong half-year FY25 result, with net profit after tax climbing 41.3% to $42.5 million. The result was largely pre-guided from a trading update on 24 January.

Audinate – Shares rallied another 7.4% after a 26.5% rally on Monday. Jefferies upgraded the stock to a Buy (from Hold) and raised its target price to $12 (from $9) in response to yesterday's half-year result.

A2 Milk – A2 Milk has also received a notable broker upgrade, with its shares up 2.6% after a 19.6% jump on Monday. Macquarie analysts upgraded the stock to Overweight (from Neutral) and raised their target price to $7.85 (from $5.70).

Resimac – Shares tumbled 14% after the non-bank lender reported a 12% decline in unaudited 1H25 net profit after tax to $15 million, citing 'a significant increase in collective provisioning'. The company noted a rise in arrears within its asset finance portfolio, with net write-offs surging to $6.5 million from $2.9 million a year ago. As a result, management believes it is "probable" that the Group will fall short of its NPAT expectations for FY25.

Challenger – Challenger's shares fell 10% following the release of its half-year results. Despite a 12% increase in normalised net profit after tax to $225 million, the company's statutory net profit after tax of $72 million and a weaker COE margin disappointed some analysts.

Bapcor – Shares fell 6.3% after the company's Chief Financial Officer George Saoud tendered his resignation to pursue an opportunity external to the company. His departure comes ahead of the company's 1H25 result, set for release on Wednesday, 26 February.

Baby Bunting – Shares eased 2.7% after the company delivered a first-half result that was largely in line with guidance, with net profit rising 37.3% to $4.8 million. A key surprise was the Board’s decision to withhold an interim dividend to support the Group’s capital expenditure and growth initiatives. This contrasts with Morgans' expectation of a 2.3 cents per share payout.

data.title: Helios Energy Appoints Edward J May as Chief Financial Officer, Shares Up 9%
data.description:
Helios Energy has appointed Edward J May as the chief financial officer, effective immediately, according to a Tuesday filing with the Australian bourse.

May has 20 years of accounting and financial experience in the oil and gas industry and owns his own oil and gas accounting services firm based in the US, the filing added.

Company shares were up 9% in recent trade.

data.title: Japan's Nikkei gains as investors pick up banks and defence shares
data.description:
Japan's Nikkei share average rose on Tuesday, as investors cherry-picked banks on interest rate-hike bets and defence stocks after their European peers closed higher overnight.

The Nikkei had climbed 0.3% to 39,296.11 by the midday break, while the broader Topix was up 0.2% at 2,772.78.

U.S. markets were closed on Monday for a holiday and the yen traded mostly unchanged on Tuesday, leaving Japanese stocks to trade in recent ranges as share- and sector-specific drivers drew the biggest moves.

Mitsubishi Heavy Industries and other defence-related shares rallied, after their European peers gained on Monday as investors priced in the likelihood of increased military spending in the region.

Mitsubishi Heavy Industries rose 2.8%, Kawasaki Heavy Industries climbed 3.4% and IHI Corp jumped 4.6%.

Rate-sensitive banks made modest gains again after data showed on Monday the economy expanded more than expected in the fourth quarter, supporting the Bank of Japan's (BOJ) plans to continue hiking rates.

Market attention will be on remarks by BOJ board member Hajime Takata on Wednesday, followed by the CPI report on Friday.

If the inflation report produces a strong number, that may lead to bets that the BOJ will hike again sooner than currently expected, strengthening the yen, said Masahiro Ichikawa, chief market strategist at Sumitomo Mitsui DS Asset Management.

"If the yen appreciates significantly, that could put pressure on Japanese stocks and potentially trigger selling," he said.

Mizuho Financial Group gained 0.8%, Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group was up 0.4%, and Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group rose 0.9%.

Nissan Motor surged 5.4% after the Financial Times reported rival Honda Motor is ready to revive merger talks if Nissan CEO Makoto Uchida steps down. Honda ticked up 0.3%.

Chip-related shares Advantest and Tokyo Electron added 2.5% and 1.8%, respectively, to give the Nikkei the biggest lift.

data.title: NauticAWT Draws Closer to SGX Delisting
data.description:
NauticAWT received in-principle approval from the SGX for the company's proposed delisting, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Monday.

The approval is subject to the company meeting certain listing requirements and the immediate disclosure of the receipt of the approval for the proposed delisting.

data.title: BHP Sanguine on Tariff Threat to Canada Potash Bet — Update
data.description:
By Rhiannon Hoyle

BHP Group expects to find other buyers for potash from a new mine that it is building in Canada if the U.S. follows through on a threat to hit its neighbor with tariffs, saying American farmers would be the ones hurt by the policy.

The world's biggest miner by market value is spending more than $10 billion to build its Jansen potash project in Canada's Saskatchewan province, betting the fertilizer ingredient will be in high demand in the decades ahead, as food consumption increases.

The potash project reflects a shift by the mining giant away from fossil fuels to investments in commodities that it expects will benefit from population growth, rising living standards and the transition to a low-carbon economy. Copper is the company's other investment priority.

Chief Executive Mike Henry said it remains unclear how potash sales from Canada to the U.S. might be affected by U.S. President Trump's aggressive trade agenda, while adding that he is confident in BHP's ability to source buyers elsewhere if required.

"To the extent that there was a constraint on the flow of Canadian potash into the U.S., then we would expect to see the global market reorder," Henry told reporters on Tuesday. "We're confident we'll be able to ship all of our potash tons and that the impact on the business would not be material," he said.

The U.S. imports 80% of its potash from Canada, so any restrictions that make it more expensive would hit American farmers, said Henry.

For BHP, he said there is still some time to monitor how trade policies evolve ahead of the Jansen operations, which the company expects to be producing potash by the end of 2026.

Henry's comments on the threats of tariffs from the U.S. come after BHP reported a jump in first-half net profit, but dialed back its dividend, with weaker commodity prices eroding underlying earnings.

The Melbourne-based miner said it made a net profit of $4.42 billion in the six months through December. That compared to a profit of $927 million a year ago, when the company wrote down the value of its nickel operations and set aside more cash to cover costs associated with the 2015 Samarco disaster.

Underlying profit, a closely watched measure of profitability that strips out some one-time charges, fell by 23% to $5.08 billion. Directors of the company declared an interim dividend of 50 cents a share, down from 72 cents a share a year ago.

Prices for the steelmaking commodities that BHP sells, such as iron ore, have weakened amid concerns over growth. China's property market, a big user of steel, has been in turmoil because of a massive glut.

Henry said there are early signs of a recovery in China and that India continues to be a standout for commodities demand. However, trade tensions could threaten a recovery in developed economies and more broadly, worldwide, according to BHP.

"Nobody quite knows where things are going to land," Henry said.

"The focus is more on the overall level of uncertainty that's created, as the U.S. makes its determinations around what tariffs to put in place, and as you see some countries talking about counter tariffs," he said.

Trade wars risk reverberating through the world market for iron ore, which accounted for 56% of BHP's underlying earnings in the half-year period. BHP sells the vast majority of its iron ore to China, to be turned into steel.

"Depending on how tariffs and counter tariffs play out, if that slowed the rate of global growth, there could be some impact on all commodities, inclusive of iron ore," Henry said.

BHP is currently studying a further expansion of its mammoth Australian iron-ore mining operations. Henry said the outlook for the global economy will help determine whether BHP goes ahead with that expansion.

The direct impacts on BHP's business from planned U.S. tariffs are not significant, he said, "We only have about 3% of the company's revenue into the U.S."

One potential win for BHP could be at the Resolution copper project in Arizona that it jointly owns with Anglo-Australian miner Rio Tinto. The project has been beset by legal challenges because of opposition from a group of local Apaches.

According to BHP, it is one of the largest undeveloped copper projects in the world, with the potential to become a significant copper producer in North America.

"By all accounts, the Trump administration is supportive of more mining domestically in the U.S.," Henry said.

U.S. copper prices have also recently climbed, fanned by the talk of tariffs, "which all bodes well then for the potential to develop Resolution," he said.

BHP has been betting on a strong outlook for copper demand, which it expects to rise by roughly 70% by 2050. Copper is a key component of electric vehicles, renewable energy and grid infrastructure.

In January, BHP formed a joint venture with Lundin Mining to develop the Filo del Sol and Josemaria projects in Argentina, which it says appears to be one of the most significant global copper discoveries in decades. Henry said that it could become a top 10 source of copper globally.

Last year, BHP sought to buy Anglo American with a roughly $50 billion takeover bid, but was rebuffed by directors at the rival firm. BHP is now "100% focused" on developing its own projects, rather than expanding via acquisitions, Henry said.

"In the current market, it is increasingly challenging to do large global M&A for value, for shareholder value, and that's what BHP is about at the end of the day," he said.

Write to Rhiannon Hoyle at rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com

data.title: Market Chatter: Goldman Sachs Boosts China Stock Targets
data.description:
Goldman Sachs on Monday increased its 12-month target for the CSI300 index to 4,700 from 4,600 and for MSCI China to 85 from 75, Reuters reported on the same day.

The investment bank cited artificial intelligence adoption as a key driver for earnings growth and potential $200 billion inflows, according to the report.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Key Metrics Indicate Toncoin Accumulation Continues Despite Price Struggle
data.description:
After a steady decline, Toncoin (TON) has seen a slight price increase over the past day, rising by 1.7% to $3.85. This movement comes amidst ongoing discussions about its longer-term performance and accumulation trends.

According to an analysis by CryptoQuant analyst Shiven Moodley, there are indications that TON holders are positioning for a potential rebound.

Moodley’s observations, which are detailed in a recent post on CryptoQuant’s QuickTake platform, suggest that the asset may be entering an accumulation phase despite its recent downward trajectory.

Toncoin Shows Signs of Accumulation

Moodley points to several metrics as evidence. The 180-day Sharpe Ratio, a measure of risk-adjusted returns, signals a period of accumulation. This is further supported by stable TVL (Total Value Locked) in lending protocols and a noticeable reduction in speculative trading activity.

Notably, the asset’s volatility has declined since the price spikes in December 2024 and February 2025. If this trend persists, it could imply that selling pressure is diminishing, potentially paving the way for a future rebound.

Key on-chain indicators also paint a picture of potential opportunity. The Normalized Risk Metric (NRM), which evaluates TON’s valuation relative to historical moving averages, highlights accumulation at a price level of $3.82.

Additionally, record lows in the Long-Term NRM suggest that longer-term holders are increasingly accumulating TON at these levels. Historically, similar setups have preceded market recoveries, giving investors a reason to believe that a medium-term price reversal may be on the horizon.

Moodley wrote:

It remains to be seen whether TON’s price action can stage a full recovery. However, long-term accumulation traders are best positioned to benefit from macro policy changes that could shift sentiment in the broader crypto market. The conditions could align for a potential rebound with selling pressure fading and risk metrics signalling a low-risk environment.

On-Chain Metrics Hint at Long-Term Opportunity

Another metric Moodley pointed out is the Risk Exposure Ratio—which tracks leveraged positions within TON’s DeFi ecosystem—it has recently reached a new high, exceeding 0.24 in early 2025. This suggests a growing influence of leveraged activity.

However, if the ratio begins to decline, it could indicate a stabilization in market conditions, potentially leading to more stable price movements.

Furthermore, the Probability of Spend metric shows that coins older than 400 days are unlikely to move, indicating strong conviction among long-term holders. This trend has historically correlated with phases of accumulation and recovery.

As Moodley notes, short- to medium-term holders appear to be exiting their positions, likely contributing to the recent price weakness. Meanwhile, long-term holders remain consistent, suggesting a belief in the asset’s long-term potential.

If selling pressure continues to ease and risk metrics improve, TON could be setting the stage for a more favorable market environment. In this scenario, long-term investors may be well-positioned to benefit from potential macroeconomic shifts that could ultimately boost Toncoin’s value.

Featured image created with DALL-E, Chart from TradingView

data.title: Can Nifty 50 reclaim 26,000 by December 2025?
data.description:
Benchmark index Nifty 50 has tumbled almost 13 percent from its all-time high recorded in September 2024. However, international brokerage Citi Research sees the frontline making up the gains to hit the 26,000-level by December 2025, indicating a 13 percent upside from current levels.

As a result of the ongoing correction, the brokerage remains constructive on the markets, as the valuations for large-cap counters turns reasonable.

Further, Citi Research noted that the trio of direct income tax cuts, repo rate cut, coupled with a decline in inflation shall boost consumption, while public capex has been on a steady uptick since December 2024.

The brokerage reiterated its 'buy' rating on HDFC Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Maruti Suzuki, Endurance Technologies, HDFC Life, Torrent Pharma, and MakeMyTrip.

On the sectoral front, Citi is overweight on banks, telecom and healthcare. On the flip side, the brokerage is underweight on IT, metals and consumer discretionary counters.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live updates

“India’s EPS growth outlook remains robust and relatively low risk given the diversified universe of listed companies,” Surendra Goyal, Strategist at Citi said in a note earlier last month. However, Citi is cautious on any recovery in the private investment climate, which is says 'could remain elusive'.

The brokerage also said government expenditure is accelerating, and fiscal consolidation in FY26 is expected to be milder than in FY25. Meanwhile, the RBI is prioritising liquidity and reducing regulatory burdens, addressing key factors behind the recent slowdown.

Retail investors have also shown resilience, reinforcing the case for a market rebound. However, risks remain, including potential policy missteps, a deepening US market correction, and slower global growth. Despite these uncertainties, improving fundamentals set the stage for a broad market recovery in the near term, it had said.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Higher Govt Spending Could Drive A-Share Market Re-Rating for China — Market Talk
data.description: Higher government spending driven by a larger fiscal deficit and a rebound in credit due to reserve requirement ratio and rate cuts may drive an A-share re-rating, UBS analysts write in a note. The government is expected to release more specific policy support at its Two Sessions meeting in early March, they add. Under the country's regulatory guidelines, regulators are guiding a structural shift to a more investor oriented A-share market featuring less financing and shareholding reductions by major shareholders, they say. Structurally, the equity risk premium could be reduced by boosting consumption, facilitating non-state-owned enterprises development and stabilizing foreign investment, UBS says.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0302 GMT - Higher government spending driven by a larger fiscal deficit and a rebound in credit due to reserve requirement ratio and rate cuts may drive an A-share re-rating, UBS analysts write in a note. The government is expected to release more specific policy support at its Two Sessions meeting in early March, they add. Under the country's regulatory guidelines, regulators are guiding a structural shift to a more investor oriented A-share market featuring less financing and shareholding reductions by major shareholders, they say. Structurally, the equity risk premium could be reduced by boosting consumption, facilitating non-state-owned enterprises development and stabilizing foreign investment, UBS says.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0257 GMT - Chinese President Xi Jinping's meeting with leading tech and internet industry executives should bode well for the sector's valuation, Citi analysts say in a research note. The meeting indicates the importance of technology innovation and the contribution of private enterprises to China's economic growth, the analysts say. The participants' list, including Alibaba founder Jack Ma and DeepSeek founder Liang Wenfeng, showcases the importance of the internet, tech, AI and EV sectors, they say. "The call for entrepreneurs to show their talents and unify their thoughts likely symbolizes the need to unite together to defend any macro headwinds and external challenges like the geopolitical tension," they add. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0254 GMT - SK Innovation's earnings before interest and taxes may rise by 30% this year after the merging of its profitable sister companies--a city-gas supplier and a liquefied-natural-gas independent power producer, Nomura analyst Cindy Park writes in a note. Park nearly triples her 2025 net-profit forecast for the South Korean energy company to KRW776 billion. But the company's battery business may continue to post losses in 2025, while earnings growth at its refining and chemical businesses may remain limited, she adds. Nomura raises its target price for the stock by 8.3% to KRW130,000 and maintains a neutral rating. Shares are 0.7% lower at KRW127,600. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0248 GMT - iFAST stands to benefit from more contributions by its ePension division in 2025, UOB Kay Hian analysts say in a research report. The division has been ramping up onboarding of trustees, with five of 24 schemes onboarded as of Dec. 13, the analysts note. For 2025, 12 are tentatively scheduled for 1Q or 2Q, two for 2Q or 3Q and the remaining five for 3Q or 4Q. For the investment product distribution platform's iFAST Global Bank, management expects full-year of profitability this year, aided by quickened growth from net interest income as bank's deposit base expands. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to S$8.30 from S$8.17 with an unchanged hold rating. Shares are unchanged at S$8.07. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0240 GMT - The a2 Milk Company's bull at Citi sees the infant-formula maker's December-half result as more evidence that headwinds in China against overseas providers have dissipated. Gone are the days in which market-share gains are dominated by domestic brands, analyst Sam Teeger says. Reiterating his buy recommendation, Teeger tells clients in a note that a2 Milk is the third highest market-share gainer in China, trailing Nestle and China's Yili. Three of the top five brands are now from outside China, he observes. He sees a2 Milk gearing up for a strong fiscal 2026, with share-price catalysts including potential government stimulus, supply chain acquisitions, and capital management. Citi raises its target price 12% to A$8.20. Shares are up 2.7% at A$7.315. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0237 GMT - Bermaz Auto could face significant challenges as its car offerings, particularly Mazda and Kia, struggle with declining sales, Affin Hwang IB says in a note. The introduction of more affordable alternatives from Chinese brands and RON95 fuel-subsidy reforms, which could weigh on consumer spending, lead her to cut the Malaysian auto distributor's FY 2025-FY 2027 core EPS forecasts by 17%-29%. Weak demand for certain Kia models and continuing promotions are also pressuring margins, she adds. Affin Hwang reduces Bermaz Auto's target price to MYR1.05 from MYR1.89 while maintaining a sell rating on the stock. Shares are 0.8% lower at MYR1.25. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0235 GMT - Jefferies analysts aren't ruling out more copper M&A for BHP, even after the miner's CEO, Mike Henry, said the company is firmly focused on its own projects. "We reiterate our hold rating on BHP as the company has been more aggressive than its peers in pursuing opportunistic M&A, and we believe further M&A risk remains," the Jefferies analysts say in a note. They also raise concerns about the outlook for dividends unless commodity prices surprise. "Combined with its rising net debt and organic growth pipeline, BHP's ability to deliver capital returns in excess of its base 50% payout ratio is limited unless commodity prices are much stronger than we anticipate," the analysts say. Jefferies has a A$43.00 target on BHP, which is up 0.5% at A$41.01/share.(rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0229 GMT - Seatrium's order book faces upside risks from President Trump's focus on U.S. energy dominance, OCBC Investment Research's Ada Lim says in a research report. Trump has laid out a plan to unleash U.S. oil and gas production and boost output, which includes promoting oil and gas development in Alaska, the analyst notes. Such policy developments could prompt global oil companies to increase oil and gas capital expenditures, potentially enabling Seatrium's order book to surprise on the upside, the analyst says. OCBC raises the stock's fair value estimate to S$2.82 from S$2.64 to reflect a valuation roll-forward, with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 0.4% higher at S$2.58. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0205 GMT - Chinese shares edge lower in early trade with the Shanghai Composite Index down 0.3%. The Shenzhen Composite Index is 0.3% lower and ChiNext Price Index is off 0.2%. Investors are waiting for China's key political meeting in early March and more economic data. Among major stocks, retail stocks are leading the declines, with Yonghui Superstores dropping 3.4%. China Tourism Group Duty Free declines 1.1%. Gainers include BYD, which adds 1.4% and CATL, which gains 1.0%. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0203 GMT - Johor Plantations' earnings outlook may stay robust, supported by stronger average selling prices for palm oil and manageable unit costs, RHB IB analyst Hoe Lee Leng says in a note. She raises Johor Plantations' 2025-2026 earnings estimates by 5% and 7%, respectively, after robust 4Q 2024 earnings. The plantation company remains an appealing investment option, trading at a relatively attractive 2025 price-to-earnings ratio of 11X, which is at the mid-point of its peers' 8X-16X, she adds. RHB raises Johor Plantations' target price to MYR1.65 from MYR1.55, while maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 0.8% higher at MYR1.21. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0153 GMT - Hong Kong's benchmark Hang Seng Index rises 0.8% to 22800.86, led by tech stocks. Chinese President Xi's meeting with top tech CEOs where he urged the business leaders to stay competitive and confident in the future of the country, sends a strong signal on China's commitment to boost the economy, says the Saxo APAC Research team in a commentary. Xiaomi is up 3.0%, BYD gains 2.1% and Alibaba Group adds 1.9%. The Hang Seng Tech Index is 1.4% higher. Among decliners, Henderson Land Development falls 1.8%, Wharf Real Estate Investment is down 1.5% and China Resources Land is 1.0% lower. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0149 GMT - Seek's first-half result is potentially higher quality than anticipated by the market even though earnings are slightly lower than the average analyst forecast, E&P analyst Entcho Raykovski says. The Australian job advertiser's December-half Ebitda of A$233.9 million compares with an average forecast of A$231.2 million, although it is in line with Raykovski's forecast. He writes in a note that the company's updated Ebitda guidance is in line with expectations despite some shift in spending from capital expenditure to operating expenses. With yield growth guidance upgraded, Raykovski sees reasons to be positive on the quality of the result. E&P has a last-published positive rating and A$28.30 target price on the stock, which is flat at A$24.23. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Australia's MLG Oz tumbles on narrowing HY profit
data.description:
** Shares of MLG Oz fall 12.3% to A$0.605

** Stock fell as much as 19.6% to A$0.555 in early trade, its largest intraday pct drop since early June 2021

** Mining services provider posts net profit of A$4.1 million for HY2025, down 42.7% from A$7.1 mln logged a year ago

** Attributes lower profit to lower revenue in crushing and screening business, reducing overall profit margins

** Adds capex to slow over second half of fiscal 2025

** About 1.3 mln shares change hands, 8.3 times the 30-day avg

** Stock up 10% YTD, including current session's moves

data.title: Future Generation Australia Names Inaugural Chief Investment Officer
data.description:
Philanthropic investment group Future Generation Australia appointed Lee Hopperton as its first chief investment officer, according to a Tuesday filing with the Australian bourse.

Hopperton will work across Future Generation Australia, Future Generation Global, and the newly launched unlisted trust, Future Generation Women.

Future Generation Australia's shares were up almost 1% in recent Tuesday trade.

data.title: Golden Mile Resources Awards Maiden Drilling Contract to Alford Drilling For its US Copper Project
data.description:
Golden Mile Resources has awarded a maiden drilling contract to Alford Drilling for two prospects under its Pearl copper project in Arizona, according to a Monday filing with the Australian bourse.

The program is expected to drill 14 to 16 reverse circulation holes for up to 2,000 meters, the filing said.

The company will begin preparing the drill sites utilizing a local earth-moving contractor after receiving the remaining permits, which is expected imminently. Alford Drilling will then be mobilized on-site to begin the program, the filing added.

data.title: U.S. Trade Uncertainties Could Keep Indonesia's Trade Surplus Volatile This Year — Market Talk
data.description: Indonesia's trade balance performance may remain volatile due to uncertainties surrounding the U.S. trade war, UOB economists Enrico Tanuwidjaja and Vincentius Ming Shen say in a note. Imports could be affected by the trade conflict and signs of weakening domestic demand since mid-2024, they flag. However, with government spending rising and robust foreign direct investment inflows, imports are likely to gradually increase due to higher domestic consumption and investment, they say. UOB expects Indonesia's trade surplus to reach approximately $38 billion this year, versus $31 billion last year, but notes downside risks to its forecast. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0304 GMT - Indonesia's trade balance performance may remain volatile due to uncertainties surrounding the U.S. trade war, UOB economists Enrico Tanuwidjaja and Vincentius Ming Shen say in a note. Imports could be affected by the trade conflict and signs of weakening domestic demand since mid-2024, they flag. However, with government spending rising and robust foreign direct investment inflows, imports are likely to gradually increase due to higher domestic consumption and investment, they say. UOB expects Indonesia's trade surplus to reach approximately $38 billion this year, versus $31 billion last year, but notes downside risks to its forecast. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0256 GMT - Singapore's trade performance this year could be dented by uncertainties around U.S. trade policy and potential escalation of trade tensions, says RHB Bank's Barnabas Gan. Singapore may be less vulnerable to direct U.S. tariff risks as it is the only Asean economy with a trade deficit with the U.S., Gan writes in a note. However, Singapore's small and open economy may not be shielded from the global trade environment's negative shocks, especially if trade tensions escalate, he adds. The Singapore economy is expected to benefit from rising demand for electronics, electrical equipment and more trade activity, Gan says. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0215 GMT - The Bank of Thailand may cut its policy rate next week on economic considerations, CIMB analysts write in a note. On-year growth for 4Q edged up to 3.2%, but on a seasonally adjusted quarterly basis, growth slowed to 0.4% during the quarter from 3Q's 1.2% expansion, they note. Full-year growth was 2.5%, which fell short of the BOT's 2.7% forecast, they say. "Looking ahead, Thailand's export sector will be expected to navigate the choppy water of U.S. President Trump's protectionist policies and tariff measures," CIMB says. Some headwinds that would undermine Thailand's manufacturing sector and economic recovery include import flooding and trade spillovers from emerging markets and China, CIMB adds. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0212 GMT - Japan's economy might finally transition to a "normal economy" as price hikes, wage hikes and rate hikes become entrenched, Nomura analysts say. They raise growth forecasts for the country in the wake of preliminary estimates for 4Q 2024, expecting a gradual recovery centered on private-sector domestic demand. They think the economy and inflation are likely to become more "Japan-centered," with the main economic driver shifting from exports to domestic demand, inflation from import prices to wages, and the BOJ having more room to conduct policy independent of other central banks. Nomura sees real GDP growth at 0.8% for FY 2024, 1.0% for FY 2025 and 0.7% for FY 2026. It expects inflation to remain around 2% on an underlying basis and assumes 25bp rate increases in July 2025 and January 2026. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0206 GMT - Most Asian currencies weaken against dollar in the morning session, facing pressure from largely higher Treasury yields, which usually increases the allure of U.S. fixed-income assets and demand for the greenback. It also remains to be seen whether there'll be any concrete measures to boost China's private sector, says MUFG Bank's Michael Wan, noting Chinese President Xi's meeting key business leaders on Monday. Key catalysts to follow will likely be China's 'government work report' and the National People's Congress meeting heading into March, the senior currency analyst adds. USD/KRW edges 0.1% higher to 1,444.08; USD/SGD is up 0.1% at 1.3431; AUD/USD falls 0.2% to 0.6341. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0152 GMT - Malaysia's exports growth likely remained strong in January, though the pace is expected to have slowed on month, according to the median estimate from a Wall Street Journal poll of six economists. Exports are estimated to have risen 6.1% on year, while imports likely increased 3.2%, leading to a trade surplus of 14.1 billion ringgit. Exports grew 16.9% in December. Outbound shipments likely maintained their strong pace in January, supported by continued front-loading demand as Trump's tariff threats linger, says Kenanga IB economist Muhammad Saifuddin Sapuan. However, seasonal factors, including the Lunar New Year holidays, may dampen the near-term outlook, he adds. The trade data are due Thursday. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0146 GMT - Thailand's 2025 economic outlook continues to be pressured by rising global policy uncertainty, especially in U.S. trade policy, says J.P. Morgan economist Jin Tik Ngai in a note. So far, exporters don't seem to be taking measures that are consistent with a higher-tariff environment, such as revising long-term capex plans, he says. It appears that the business community is adopting a wait-and-see approach amid the flurry of tariff threats, he says. However, he reckons that higher U.S. tariffs are a "matter of when, not if," and the negative impact on Thailand's exports, private investment and GDP growth will likely be significant. J.P. Morgan maintains its 2025 GDP growth estimate for Thailand at 2.2%. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0134 GMT - Japan's economic growth looks set to pick up sharply to 1.3% in 2025 from 0.1%, HSBC economist Jun Takazawa says. Though spending moderated in the final quarter of 2024, HSBC attributes that more to one-off or cyclical factors like weather weighing on clothing purchases or food supply shocks weighing on sentiment. Over the medium term, broadening wage hikes should buttress the consumption recovery, the economist says. The recent meeting between PM Ishiba and Trump was also more positive than expected, providing some reprieve, he adds. The agenda included items Japan has already been moving forward with, like raising direct investment in the U.S., importing more LNG and strengthening defense ties. While much of U.S. policy remains ever-changing, Japan has considerable room for negotiation, he adds. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0108 GMT - Thailand's central bank likely faces increased pressure to cut rates as uncertainties in tourism and trade cloud the economic outlook, HSBC's Aris Dacanay says. Thailand runs large tariff rate differentials with the U.S., which is one of its top export destinations, and any tariffs risk wiping off a substantial amount of export demand, the economist says. The tourism outlook seems wobbly despite strong January visitor numbers, he adds, with no surge in Chinese arrivals. The slight on-year acceleration in private consumption in 4Q also puts into question how effective the next phases of the government's cash handout program will be. If fiscal easing doesn't deliver the growth intended, pressure for the Bank of Thailand to cut rates further will likely grow, he says. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0028 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi edges up 0.1% to 2613.46 in early trade as defense stocks advance. Retail investors are net buyers. Howitzer and aircraft-engine maker Hanwha Aerospace climbed 11%, extending gains another day as it showcased its long-range surface-to-air missile system at this week's defense exhibition in Abu Dhabi. Local defense stocks are largely higher. Index heavyweight Samsung Electronics is up 0.5% following its announcement to cancel around KRW3 trillion of shares Thursday. USD/KRW is flat at 1,441.40 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield is up 2.3 bps at 2.869%.(kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0025 GMT - Asian currencies consolidate against the dollar in the early morning session, but may strengthen as small gains in U.S. stock-index futures imply mild risk appetite. Also, the greenback could weaken further this week as additional U.S. tariffs may be delayed by several months, CBA's Kristina Clifton says in a research report. "The longer the delay, the more time businesses and governments have available to secure exemptions from the U.S. government," the senior economist and currency strategist says. USD/KRW is little changed at 1,441.85; USD/SGD is steady at 1.3417; USD/CNH is little changed at 7.2660. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0015 GMT - AUD/USD may rise as much as 1.0% if the RBA keeps rates unchanged, CBA's Kristina Clifton says in a research report. "Our analysis of past decisions suggests AUD/USD could lift by up to 1.0% 30 minutes after the decision if the RBA leaves the cash rate at 4.35%," the senior economist and currency strategist says. How far AUD/USD climbs will depend on the RBA's post-meeting statement and press conference, Clifton says. If the statement suggests a rate cut will be delivered soon, AUD/USD's rise may be smaller. However, if the statement is non-committal about a rate cut, AUD/USD could rise by more than 1%, Clifton adds. AUD/USD is 0.1% lower at 0.6351. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Lithium Resources Appoints Chairman
data.description:
Global Lithium Resources appointed Richard O'Shannassy as its independent, non-executive director and chairman, according to a Tuesday filing with the Australian bourse.

O'Shannassy has more than 40 years of experience in the resources and property sector, the filing added.

Company shares were down 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Westgold Resources Extends Gold at Western Australian Project
data.description:
Westgold Resources extended gold mineralization from an ongoing resource development drilling program at the company's Beta Hunt mine at Kambalda, Western Australia, according to a Tuesday filing with the Australian bourse.

Drilling at the Fletcher zone at the project yielded up to 41 meters at about 8 grams per tonne (g/t) grade of gold and 19 meters at roughly 6 g/t gold, the filing said.

The company expects to start drilling at the Mason zone at the project and deliver a mineral resource estimate for the Fletcher zone by the fourth quarter of the fiscal year.

Company shares rose nearly 1% in recent Tuesday trade.

data.title: Xi's Meeting with Chinese Tech CEOs Bodes Well for Sector's Valuation — Market Talk
data.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping's meeting with leading tech and internet industry executives should bode well for the sector's valuation, Citi analysts say in a research note. The meeting indicates the importance of technology innovation and the contribution of private enterprises to China's economic growth, the analysts say. The participants' list, including Alibaba founder Jack Ma and DeepSeek founder Liang Wenfeng, showcases the importance of the internet, tech, AI and EV sectors, they say. "The call for entrepreneurs to show their talents and unify their thoughts likely symbolizes the need to unite together to defend any macro headwinds and external challenges like the geopolitical tension," they add. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: SEEK's Growth Fund to Sell Part of Stake in HRM Platform for AU$75 Million
data.description:
SEEK's growth fund has agreed to sell part of its stake in HR platform Employment Hero to funds managed by investment firm KKR, according to a Tuesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The transaction will see SEEK's share of the proceeds amount to approximately AU$79 million, the filing said.

The sale reflects a 19% higher valuation for Employment Hero compared to its valuation as of June 30, 2024, the filing added.

The transaction, expected to settle by March, will help SEEK reduce debt while retaining a meaningful stake in Employment Hero.

The Fund plans to open a liquidity window in 2026, potentially generating further capital returns for SEEK, the company added.

data.title: Gold Rises for 2nd Session
data.description:
Gold rose above $2,900 per ounce on Tuesday, marking a second consecutive day of gains, as worries about a global trade war continued to underpin the bullion.

Traders remained concerned that President Donald Trump's reciprocal tariff plan would escalate global trade tensions, reinforcing bullion's appeal as a safe-haven asset.

However, hawkish remarks from Federal Reserve officials capped further gains.

Fed Governor Michelle Bowman reiterated her cautious stance on further interest rate cuts in the face of persistent inflation.

Similarly, Fed Governor Christopher Waller favored pausing rate cuts until inflationary pressures subside.

Investors are now eagerly awaiting the release of the minutes from the Fed's last meeting, due on Wednesday, which could offer more details on the policy outlook.

On the geopolitical front, investors continue to monitor developments of a possible Russia-Ukraine ceasefire agreement.

data.title: Palm falls on weaker rival oils, but soft ringgit limits losses
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures opened lower on Tuesday, pressured by lacklustre performances in rival edible oils, although a weaker ringgit limited the downturn.

The benchmark palm oil contract for May delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange slid 35 ringgit, or 0.77%, to 4,507 ringgit ($1,015.55) a metric ton in early trade.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) fell 0.61% while its palm oil contract shed 0.22%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade were down 0.67%.

* Palm oil tracks price movements of rival edible oils, as they compete for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

* The ringgit , palm's currency of trade, weakened 0.14% against the dollar, making the commodity cheaper for buyers holding foreign currencies.

* Indonesia has set a target to replant 120,000 hectares of smallholders' palm oil plantations this year, the chief executive of the state plantation fund said.

* Brazil's soybean harvest for the 2024/25 season reached 23% of the planted area, as of last Thursday, up 8 percentage points from the previous week, while 32% of the total area had been reaped, agribusiness consultancy AgRural said.

* Brent crude oil prices paused in early trading, after posting gains in the previous session following a drone attack on an oil pipeline pumping station in Russia that reduced flows from Kazakhstan. O/R

* Palm oil may break resistance at 4,548 ringgit per metric ton and rise into the 4,593-4,633 ringgit range, Reuters technical analyst Wang Tao said. TECH/C

MARKET NEWS

* Asian shares and the dollar steadied, as traders waited on a rate cut in Australia and company earnings in China, while European shares marked record highs on the prospect of ramping up defence spending to back up any Ukraine peace deal. MKTS/GLOB

DATA/EVENTS

0700 UK Claimant Count Unem Chng, HMRC Payrolls Change Jan

0700 UK ILO Unemployment Rate Dec

0745 France CPI (EU Norm) Final MM, CPI (EU Norm) Final YY Jan

0745 France CPI YY NSA, CPI MM NSA Jan

1000 Germany ZEW Economic Sentiment, ZEW Current Conditions Feb

($1 = 4.4380 ringgit)

data.title: DomaCom Secures Nearly AU$2 Million Private Placement
data.description:
DomaCom secured a AU$1.5 million private placement from MFL Properties, allowing the latter to subscribe for 107.1 million shares at AU$0.014 apiece, according to a Monday filing with the Australian bourse.

MFL Properties will be issued 50 million options exercisable on Dec. 12, 2025, at AU$0 as part of the placement if the 30-day volume weighted average price on that date is less than AU$0.02, the filing said. If the price is AU$0.02 or higher, the options will be canceled.

Funds raised will be used for initiatives such as enhancing the company's technology platform, expanding operational capabilities, and launching alternative investment solutions, per the filing.

data.title: South Korea Financial Supervisory Service: Funds Raised in IPOs Increased Slightly in 2024
South Korea FSS: Investor Sentiment Deteriorated in 2H Amid Uncertainty, Stock Market Volatility
South Korea FSS: IPO Proceeds Totaled KRW3.9T in 2024 Vs KRW3.3T in 2023
South Korea FSS: 77 Companies Went Public in 2024 Vs 82 in 2023
Goodwill Entertainment Unit Forms JV for Multi-Entertainment Outlet in Kuala Lumpur
data.description:
Goodwill Entertainment's wholly-owned subsidiary, Have Fun Lite, partnered with HF Entertainment to establish a joint venture company in Malaysia.

HF Entertainment is owned by Singaporean DJ Cher Ng, Oversea Enterprise , and Luk Hing Entertainment Group .

The JV will invest, operate, and manage a project of a multi-entertainment outlet that includes karaoke, live music performances, and food and beverage services in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.

Under the agreement, the parties have agreed to an additional capital injection to start the project.

Estimated costs for the project are around 20 million ringgit, with funding by HF Lite and HF Entertainment to be carried out in 70:30 proportions through a proposed subscription of 1.0 million ringgit and the additional capital injection of 19.0 million ringgit.

The investment will be made through internal resources, according to a Monday filing.
